findings.evidence: Line 349 declares `private def StronglyContinuousSemigroup.genQuot`; lines 437-440 expose `Filter.Tendsto (S.genQuot (x : X)) ...`.
findings.fix: Restate `generator_tendsto` with the explicit function `fun t => (1 / t) • (S.operator t (x : X) - (x : X))`, and add the matching explicit domain introduction/iff lemma.
findings.issue: `StronglyContinuousSemigroup.generator_tendsto` is the public characteristic lemma for the generator, but its statement mentions the private helper `S.genQuot`, so downstream users cannot use the API without an inaccessible implementation name.

findings.evidence: Lines 402-421 publish `genMap` solely to fill `generator.toFun`; no roadmap target or later public theorem consumes `genMap` directly.
findings.fix: Make `genMap` private or inline it into `generator`, and keep the public interface through `generator`, `domain`, and characteristic lemmas.
findings.issue: `StronglyContinuousSemigroup.genMap` exposes the chosen underlying linear map of the generator even though the public object is `S.generator`; this is implementation surface, not target API.

findings.evidence: `rg "ContractionSemigroup\.resolventRightInv|resolventRightInv"` finds only the general theorem at line 761, while the contraction specialization block at lines 791-812 exposes the other resolvent API.
findings.fix: Add `ContractionSemigroup.resolventRightInv` as the `(ω,M)=(0,1)` specialization of `StronglyContinuousSemigroup.resolventRightInv`, stated using `S.resolvent`.
findings.issue: The contraction resolvent wrapper has `resolvent_apply`, `resolventMapsToDomain`, and `resolvent_norm_le`, but lacks the parallel `resolventRightInv` theorem for `(λ - A) R λ = I`.

summary: The new semigroup surface is close, but the generator API is not usable without private helpers and one implementation helper is exposed. The contraction resolvent wrapper is also missing the right-inverse characteristic lemma developed for the general resolvent.
